error.signature: connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream

verdict.summary: nginx cannot reach the backend. Backend not running, wrong port, or listening on different interface.

dead_ends.action: Change proxy_pass to use hostname instead of IP
dead_ends.why_fails: DNS resolution adds latency and can fail; use IP for local backends

dead_ends.action: Restart nginx assuming the issue is with nginx
dead_ends.why_fails: nginx is working correctly by reporting the error; the backend is the problem

workarounds.action: Verify the backend is running and listening on the expected port
workarounds.how: curl -v http://127.0.0.1:8000  # test backend directly; ss -tlnp | grep 8000

workarounds.action: Check backend is listening on correct interface (0.0.0.0 vs 127.0.0.1)
workarounds.how: If backend listens on 127.0.0.1 but nginx uses proxy_pass to container IP, connection refused

workarounds.action: Check firewall rules and SELinux if enabled
workarounds.how: sudo setsebool -P httpd_can_network_connect 1  # SELinux blocks nginx upstream by default
